reasoning: The criteria require the 30-day count of "Violence against civilians" to exceed 10x the reference value. The reference value as of Jan 22, 2026 was 199.5. This means there would need to be 1,995 events in 30 days. As established by ACLED's June 2025 update, even after a large expansion to include gang violence, they only added 4,537 "Violence against civilians" events spread across 2.5 years (Jan 2023 to June 2025). The 30-day average is now around 200. To jump from 200/month to 1,995/month is physically and practically impossible outside of a sudden descent into a major war covering the entire country in multiple coordinated massacres every single day, which unequivocally is not happening in early 2026. Therefore, the chance of this hitting >10x the baseline is basically 0%. I assign 2% and 3% to account for some completely unpredictable ACLED methodology error/glitch that randomly dumps past events into a single month.
